project.name: VibeCodeMap
project.summary: Experimental evidence-model and browser-map toolchain. A Go CLI inventories repositories, runs prototype language analyzers, maps deterministic measurements into quality DSL, validates editable VCM DSL, composes a renderer-neutral view model, and opens a Three.js condition map. AI agents author architecture; the CLI does not invent systems.

cities.name: VibeCodeMap
cities.summary: Research prototype that turns source-linked semantic models into an interactive 3D condition map for architecture and evidence review.

districts.name: CLI surface
districts.summary: Process entrypoint and command routing for developers and agents.

districts.name: Inventory and scope
districts.summary: Central repository scan and four-action reading policy.

districts.name: Language adapters
districts.summary: Stack detection and prototype semantic analyzers for Go, Python, and JS/TS.

districts.name: Quality and presentation
districts.summary: Evidence-to-quality conversion, DSL validation, composition, road aggregation, and browser map generation.

districts.name: Agent guidance
districts.summary: Skill and agent instructions that perform semantic mapping the CLI does not.

nodes.name: Developer
nodes.summary: Human who builds the CLI, reviews maps, and corrects editable VCM DSL.

nodes.name: AI mapping agent
nodes.summary: Agent that uses inspect/analyze evidence plus source reading to author source-linked DSL.

nodes.name: vibecodemap CLI
nodes.summary: Single Go binary that owns inventory, analyzer orchestration, evidence-to-quality conversion, validation, composition, HTML generation, and browser launch.

nodes.name: CLI command router
nodes.summary: Dispatches describe, schema, validate, inspect, analyze, quality, adapters, render, and show.

nodes.name: Repository inventory
nodes.summary: Walks a repository once, applies scope policy and gitignore, and emits an auditable inventory report.

nodes.name: Scope policy
nodes.summary: Classifies paths as analyze, summarize, externalize, or ignore using defaults, markers, and optional .vcmignore.

nodes.name: Adapter registry
nodes.summary: Registers detectors/analyzers, runs detection, and builds evidence bundles without inventing architecture.

nodes.name: Go AST analyzer
nodes.summary: Prototype Go parser/AST evidence extractor; no go/packages or type checking yet.

nodes.name: Python AST analyzer
nodes.summary: Go-orchestrated embedded Python AST subprocess over centrally approved .py files.

nodes.name: JS/TS lexical analyzer
nodes.summary: Dependency-free lexical prototype for JavaScript and TypeScript; not a compiler AST.

nodes.name: Quality evidence bridge
nodes.summary: Converts supported analyzer facts into source-linked quality DSL while preserving unavailable metrics as unknown.

nodes.name: Project DSL validation
nodes.summary: Validates project manifests against embedded grammar/schema and reference invariants.

nodes.name: Viewer composition pipeline
nodes.summary: Loads validated models, composes cities/districts/buildings/roads, writes view JSON and HTML, and opens a browser.

nodes.name: Affinity and road aggregation
nodes.summary: Explainable affinity metrics and deterministic district-road aggregation used by composition.

nodes.name: analyze-with-vibecodemap skill
nodes.summary: Repository-owned agent instructions for end-to-end mapping, evidence discipline, and show completion.

nodes.name: Python 3 runtime
nodes.summary: Optional host runtime required only when analyzing Python source.

nodes.name: esm.sh Three.js
nodes.summary: CDN module load used by the browser map; offline fallback shows embedded data cards.

nodes.name: Evidence bundle
nodes.summary: Intermediate analyze output at .vibecodemap/generated/evidence.json; not architecture DSL.

nodes.name: Quality model
nodes.summary: Validated quality DSL generated from path- and symbol-matched deterministic evidence, including explicit unknown measurements.

nodes.name: Generated HTML map
nodes.summary: Standalone HTML plus view JSON under .vibecodemap/out/ after show or render.

nodes.name: Deterministic workload discovery
nodes.summary: Automated product/deployable discovery from manifests and entrypoints is planned but not implemented as a CLI output.

relations.summary: When go-module is detected, the Go AST analyzer emits per-file evidence events.
When JS or TS files are detected, the lexical analyzer emits prototype evidence.
When python is detected and python3 is available, the embedded adapter runs as a subprocess.
Agent behavior is constrained by the checked-in skill and AGENTS truth boundaries.
Mapping skill orchestrates CLI inspect, analyze, and show around source investigation and DSL authoring.
inspect detects stacks; analyze builds the evidence bundle through the registry.
inspect and analyze invoke a single repository Scan with shared scope options.
The quality command validates the structural model and invokes deterministic evidence-to-quality conversion.
render and show validate models, compose the view, write HTML and JSON, and optionally open a browser.
analyze writes the intermediate evidence bundle for AI or human DSL authors.
Developer runs the vibecodemap CLI for inspect, analyze, validate, and show.
Interactive 3D runtime currently loads Three.js from esm.sh; data-card fallback remains without it.
Inventory evaluates every path against the compiled scope policy before reading contents.
Python analysis requires a host Python 3 executable on PATH.
Quality generation reads the centrally scoped evidence bundle and rejects mismatched repository roots.
The quality command writes and validates source-linked quality DSL.
Composition consumes the linked quality model to populate condition bands and provenance.
Composer aggregates district roads and lanes through the roads package.
Composition begins only after project validation succeeds.
Render writes view JSON and HTML under the project out directory.

boundaries.subject: component.cli-commands
boundaries.summary: Local users and agents pass repository and project paths into the CLI.

boundaries.subject: component.cli-commands
boundaries.summary: Analysis and map artifacts leave through stdout, .vibecodemap outputs, and the default browser.

boundaries.subject: component.analyzer.python
boundaries.summary: Python analysis crosses a process boundary with versioned request and event JSON.

security.summary: Inventory reads local repository paths chosen by the operator; it should remain a local trusted-path tool, not a remote multi-tenant scanner.
security.impact: Path traversal outside intended roots is primarily an operator-trust concern for this local CLI.
